title: iPSC-derived microglia mature in a chimeric in vitro brain tissue model towards age and disease-specific transcriptional states
author: Jun-Hoe, Lee
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Microglia are the primary immune cells of the brain; they are important for maintaining brain homeostasis and have been implicated in various neuropathological processes. Microglial identity and function are determined by their ontogeny and the microenvironment. Recent advances in the human induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) and organoid field represent a significant advancement regarding investigating human microglial function, but technical and conceptual challenges remain. Here we demonstrate that iPSC-derived microglial (iMic) precursors integrate, differentiate, and exhibit diverse age- and disease-related transcriptional profiles in a chimeric brain slice culture (CSC) model, combining murine organotypic brain slices with iMics. iMic precursor maturation is driven solely by the tissue environment, without the need for growth factor supplementation and we achieve a near-complete replacement with human microglia. Our findings suggest that CSCs are a versatile tool for investigating human microglia in a physiologically and cytoarchitecturally relevant environment, while maintaining the amenability of an in vitro system to investigate neuropathological processes in any neurological disease model.
